clusters.headline: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes
clusters.summary: President Trump had previously been amping up his rhetoric against Iran.

clusters.headline: Nigeria evacuates citizens from South Africa as anti-migrant sentiment rises
clusters.summary: Nigeria is the latest African state to repatriate citizens following reports of xenophobic attacks.

clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: DR Congo Referendum Bill Intensifies Constitutional Standoff
clusters.summary: [DW] Congolese lawmakers this week passed a referendum bill that could lead to constitutional reform ahead of the 2028 elections. The opposition has warned of a "constitutional coup."

clusters.headline: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, dancing, and protests
clusters.summary: There was a star-studded opening ceremony, and some sporadic clashes between police and protesters around the Azteca stadium.

clusters.headline: Artan to referee Uefa Super Cup after losing World Cup role
clusters.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan is appointed to take charge of the Uefa Super Cup match between Paris St-Germain and Aston Villa later this summer after being denied a spot at the World Cup.

clusters.headline: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
clusters.summary: Rights groups accuse the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in el-Obeid, a city on the civil war's front line.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Democracy Day - Tinubu Says Economic Reforms Restoring Stability, Pledges Greater Prosperity for Nigerians
clusters.summary: [This Day] President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Friday defended his administration's economic reforms, saying they were necessary to rescue Nigeria from fiscal strain and lay the foundation for long-term prosperity.

clusters.headline: South Africa: Flood-Hit Western Cape Faces Long Recovery - South African News Briefs - June 12, 2026
clusters.summary: [allAfrica] Flood-Hit Western Cape Faces Long Recovery

clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: 'We're Sitting On a Volcano' Warns EU Commissioner After Visit to Ebola-Hit DR Congo
clusters.summary: [RFI] As a deadly outbreak of Ebola continues to spread across the Democratic Republic of Congo and the wider Great Lakes region, the European Union has pledged €170m towards the health and humanitarian response. Hadja Lahbib, the European Commissioner for International Partnerships, Humanita

clusters.headline: 'They destroyed the future': Palestinian anger at rise in Israeli demolitions in East Jerusalem
clusters.summary: Palestinian homes are being pulled down in occupied East Jerusalem to make way for a park.

clusters.headline: Danantara Indonesia unit will not take over contracts in new export plan, meeting minutes show
Nigeria: Senate Passes State Police Bill, Refers Proposed Law to Constitution Review Committee
clusters.summary: [Premium Times] The bill seeks to devolve policing powers to subnational governments to strengthen Nigeria's internal security architecture.
